verses.text: The heads of the families who went up with Ezra are named and numbered, house by house. God records each company that answers the call. The returning remnant is no faceless crowd; every family that sets out for Jerusalem is known and counted by Him.

Gathering at the river, Ezra finds no Levites among the people and sends to Iddo for ministers for God's house. The work lacks those who should serve at it. A wise leader notices what is missing and seeks the servants God's worship requires.

Gathering at the river, Ezra finds no Levites among the people and sends to Iddo for ministers for God's house. The work lacks those who should serve at it. A wise leader notices what is missing and seeks the servants God's worship requires.

Gathering at the river, Ezra finds no Levites among the people and sends to Iddo for ministers for God's house. The work lacks those who should serve at it. A wise leader notices what is missing and seeks the servants God's worship requires.

By the good hand of God upon them, a wise man and a company of Levites and Nethinim are brought. The need is supplied through God's providence. When we seek the right helpers for His work, the LORD provides them.

By the good hand of God upon them, a wise man and a company of Levites and Nethinim are brought. The need is supplied through God's providence. When we seek the right helpers for His work, the LORD provides them.

Ezra proclaims a fast at Ahava, ashamed to ask the king for soldiers after telling him 'the hand of our God is upon all them for good that seek him'; they fast, and God hears. His testimony binds him to trust God alone. Faith that has boasted of God must then lean on Him in earnest.

Ezra proclaims a fast at Ahava, ashamed to ask the king for soldiers after telling him 'the hand of our God is upon all them for good that seek him'; they fast, and God hears. His testimony binds him to trust God alone. Faith that has boasted of God must then lean on Him in earnest.

Ezra proclaims a fast at Ahava, ashamed to ask the king for soldiers after telling him 'the hand of our God is upon all them for good that seek him'; they fast, and God hears. His testimony binds him to trust God alone. Faith that has boasted of God must then lean on Him in earnest.

Ezra weighs out the silver, gold, and vessels to chosen priests, charging them, 'ye are holy, and the vessels are holy; keep them.' The treasure is committed with solemn care. What is dedicated to God must be handled by faithful hands and guarded as holy.

Ezra weighs out the silver, gold, and vessels to chosen priests, charging them, 'ye are holy, and the vessels are holy; keep them.' The treasure is committed with solemn care. What is dedicated to God must be handled by faithful hands and guarded as holy.

Ezra weighs out the silver, gold, and vessels to chosen priests, charging them, 'ye are holy, and the vessels are holy; keep them.' The treasure is committed with solemn care. What is dedicated to God must be handled by faithful hands and guarded as holy.

The priests receive the weighed treasure to carry safely to the house of God. The trust is openly given and openly received. Accountability in sacred things guards both the gift and the giver from reproach.

The priests receive the weighed treasure to carry safely to the house of God. The trust is openly given and openly received. Accountability in sacred things guards both the gift and the giver from reproach.

The priests receive the weighed treasure to carry safely to the house of God. The trust is openly given and openly received. Accountability in sacred things guards both the gift and the giver from reproach.

They set out, and God delivers them from enemies and ambush on the road; arriving, they weigh the treasure into the temple. The God they trusted brings them through. He who is sought in faith proves Himself a faithful keeper of His people and their charge.

The returned exiles offer burnt offerings to the LORD and deliver the king's commissions to the officials, who further the people and the house of God. Worship and duty are both fulfilled. God's providence smooths the way for those who come home to serve Him.
